data.title: As a brainstorm agent, I can evaluate whether an epic's success criteria cover all consumers of its shared artifacts
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a consumer_completeness dimension to the existing Scope fidelity reviewer sub-agent in the scrutiny pipeline. This dimension evaluates whether the epic's success criteria cover all consumers discovered by Part C's codebase scan.

**Why**: Without an independent reviewer dimension, uncovered consumers flagged by Part C are only visible in the gap analysis output. Adding consumer_completeness as a blocking dimension on the Scope reviewer ensures that epics with incomplete consumer coverage cannot pass fidelity review, preventing the frame-bias that allowed 8df3-61d0 to ship with missing coverage.

**Scope**:
- IN: Adding consumer_completeness as a new dimension on the EXISTING Scope reviewer (scope.md); the dimension receives raw Part C scan output (file paths and matching context); consumer_completeness uses the same pass threshold (>=4) as all other fidelity dimensions — a low score blocks the epic; updating the scrutiny pipeline to pass Part C output to the Scope reviewer
- OUT: Creating a new standalone reviewer agent; modifying other existing reviewer dimensions; changes to Part C scan logic (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Scope fidelity reviewer evaluates a consumer_completeness dimension using raw Part C scan output (file paths and matching context, not brainstorm-agent summaries)
  <- Satisfies: "The Scope fidelity reviewer gains a consumer_completeness dimension"
- When this story is complete, a low consumer_completeness score (below 4) blocks the epic from passing fidelity review, using the same pass threshold as all other fidelity dimensions
  <- Satisfies: "consumer_completeness has the same pass threshold (>=4) as all other fidelity dimensions"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] consumer_completeness dimension must receive the same raw tuple format produced by Part C — coordinate on contract
- [Reliability] Large consumer lists may challenge sub-agent context limits — the reviewer should handle truncated input gracefully
